data.title: William Tng Raises AEM Price Target to S$10.15 and Reiterates Buy on Strong Earnings Upcycle and AI-Driven Growth
data.description:
Analyst William Tng from CGS International reiterated a Buy rating on AEM Holdings Ltd. and increased the price target to S$10.15 from S$4.86.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

William Tng has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, chiefly the onset of a strong earnings upcycle supported by a broader and higher-quality customer mix. He highlights that partnerships such as the one with ASE Technology, together with new AI/HPC and memory clients, should drive AEM toward the upper end of its revenue guidance and support meaningful margin expansion through FY28.

Tng also argues that AEM’s earnings growth profile, with an expected FY25–28 EPS compound annual growth rate of 83%, justifies a re-rating of the stock’s valuation multiple to be in line with sector peers. On this basis, he lifts his target price to S$10.15, seeing further upside if major customers accelerate equipment orders, while acknowledging risks from potential order delays, macroeconomic weakness, and ongoing litigation.

In another report released on April 24, DBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a S$4.60 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Hold on Neptune, Sees Balanced Risk-Reward with Price Target Maintained at $25
data.description:
Bob Huang, an analyst from Morgan Stanley, maintained the Hold rating on Neptune Insurance Holdings, Inc. Class A. The associated price target remains the same with $25.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bob Huang has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including Neptune’s clear technological edge as an AI-first insurance platform and the belief that much of this advantage is already reflected in the current share price. The company’s end-to-end integration of automation, data, and machine learning supports faster and more efficient underwriting and distribution, which should help it extend its lead in the flood insurance segment over time.

At the same time, with the stock trading near his view of fair value and the price target maintained at $25, he sees a balanced risk‑reward profile rather than a compelling entry point. Potential upside from regulatory shifts that favor private flood insurers and further AI-driven gains is offset by execution and competitive risks, leading him to recommend holding existing positions rather than aggressively buying at current levels.

According to TipRanks, Huang is an analyst with an average return of -4.3% and a 51.85% success rate. Huang covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Progressive, Marsh & McLennan Companies, and Lemonade.

In another report released on April 24, Mizuho Securities also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $29.00 price target.
